heroTitle: Storefront composition is now real, while the home page stays modular.
heroDescription: The shell, CMS, catalog, cart, and checkout slices are now live against Darwin.WebApi. Home stays intentionally light, but it is no longer a single blank-state block.
heroHighlightTheme: Theme tokens and shell chrome stay outside feature slices.
heroHighlightComposition: Home sections are composed as explicit web parts instead of a one-off layout.
metricsTitle: Current public delivery is measurable, not a hidden starter shell.
metricsDescription: These numbers come from the same live public CMS/catalog contracts and runtime configuration that the storefront currently uses.
metricPagesNote: total items currently visible through the public CMS list
metricPagesDegradedNote: public CMS list is currently degraded with status {status}
metricProductsNote: total items currently visible through the public catalog list
metricProductsDegradedNote: public catalog list is currently degraded with status {status}
metricCategoriesNote: public category entries currently available for browsing
metricCategoriesDegradedNote: public category list is currently degraded with status {status}
metricCulturesNote: active front-office cultures currently configured at runtime
contractRailTitle: Public routes should expose contract health, not hide it.
contractRailDescription: This web part keeps the current CMS, catalog, and account-entry surfaces visible as actionable storefront lanes instead of leaving runtime state buried in one hero panel.
contractCmsHealthyDescription: The public CMS list is responding cleanly enough to drive navigation, spotlight sections, and public content browsing from the same published source.
contractCmsDegradedDescription: The public CMS list is currently degraded with status {status}, so storefront content should stay observable and actionable instead of silently collapsing.
contractCmsMeta: {count} pages currently loaded into the home spotlight.
contractCatalogHealthyDescription: The public catalog list is healthy enough to drive browse, product detail, cart follow-up, and home spotlight reuse from the same contract family.
contractCatalogDegradedDescription: The public catalog list is currently degraded with status {status}, so browse and merchandising follow-up should remain visible instead of pretending discovery is complete.
contractCatalogMeta: {count} products currently loaded into the home spotlight.
contractAccountDescription: Registration, sign-in, and member follow-up already live on the same shell, so front-office account entry should stay part of one system rather than a detached auth island.
contractAccountMeta: {count} runtime cultures currently supported by the front-office shell.
contractRailEmptyMessage: The contract rail is app-composed and should stay available even when individual public feeds degrade.
stageFlowTitle: The storefront should read like one staged customer journey.
stageFlowDescription: This web part keeps content, discovery, and follow-up in one deliberate sequence so Home composition communicates system flow rather than only isolated feature entry points.
stageContentDescription: The CMS surface should frame what the shopper is looking at before product discovery begins, using the same published content truth that operators manage.
stageDiscoveryTitle: Move into browse and product selection
stageDiscoveryDescription: Catalog browsing should pick up directly from that context and keep the shopper inside the same storefront language, contract set, and shell.
stageFollowUpTitle: Keep account and member follow-up connected
stageFollowUpDescription: Account entry, loyalty, orders, and invoices should remain follow-up surfaces of the same storefront system instead of a separate sub-application.
stageFollowUpMeta: Member follow-up already lives on the shared front-office shell.
stageFlowEmptyMessage: The stage-flow composition is app-owned and should stay available even when one public data source degrades.
pairPanelTitle: Content and discovery should stay in dialogue.
pairPanelDescription: This web part keeps CMS and catalog side by side so storefront storytelling and product discovery can be compared as two active surfaces of the same system.
pairCmsHealthyDescription: The storefront can already frame the public experience with published CMS pages, navigation, and long-form detail routes from the same contract family.
pairCmsDegradedDescription: The CMS side is currently degraded with status {status}, so the storefront should keep that weakness visible instead of pretending the narrative layer is complete.
pairCmsMeta: {count} CMS pages are currently loaded into the home spotlight.
pairCatalogHealthyDescription: The public catalog is already strong enough to carry category browse, product detail, cart follow-up, and merchandising reuse from the same data source.
pairCatalogDegradedDescription: The catalog side is currently degraded with status {status}, so discovery follow-up should stay explicit rather than hiding behind generic storefront chrome.
pairCatalogMeta: {count} catalog products are currently loaded into the home spotlight.
shortcutsTitle: The current public surface is already navigable end to end.
shortcutsDescription: These shortcuts represent the currently implemented foundation before richer merchandising or campaign composition is added.
shortcutCmsDescription: Open the public CMS index and validate the same published page truth that WebAdmin now manages.
shortcutCatalogDescription: Browse published categories and products directly from Darwin.WebApi without relying on starter placeholders.
shortcutAccountDescription: Registration, activation, and password-reset self-service can grow here before full authenticated member sessions arrive.
shortcutsEmptyMessage: Storefront shortcuts are app-defined and should always be available.
journeysTitle: Each public route should already behave like part of one system.
journeysDescription: These follow-up paths keep CMS, catalog, and account entry points visible with their current contract state instead of scattering those routes across unrelated placeholders.
journeyCmsDescription: Move from the home surface into the public CMS index and then into the published page detail routes managed by the same backend contract.
journeyCatalogDescription: Move directly into the public catalog list and keep the same culture-aware catalog contracts through listing and detail pages.
journeyAccountDescription: Enter registration, sign-in, and self-service/member follow-up from the same shell instead of treating auth as a separate mini-app.
journeyAccountMeta: Self-service and member portal are already on the live front-office shell.
journeysEmptyMessage: These route follow-ups are part of the application shell and should remain available.
cmsSpotlightTitle: Published pages are now available for storefront composition.
cmsSpotlightDescription: This section is fed from the public CMS page list contract, not from a hard-coded marketing stub.
cmsPageDescriptionFallback: Published storefront content available through the public CMS contract.
cmsSpotlightEmptyMessage: No published CMS pages are available for the current home spotlight.
productSpotlightTitle: Representative public products can now be surfaced on home.
productSpotlightDescription: This section deliberately uses the current public catalog contract so later merchandising can extend from real data instead of a fake hero grid.
productDescriptionFallback: Published product card delivered from the public catalog surface.
productSpotlightEmptyMessage: No published products are currently available for the home spotlight.
